source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Unauthorized release of Synthetic Based Mud
actions_taken: Well was shut-in and circulated out through the gas buster
SBM transferred from active pit to processing pits
SBM pumped back to active pit using centrifuge pumps
Observer posted to watch for sheens for 30 minutes
Two eight-inch valves on discharge side of degasser tank removed and inspected
Crews will fill processing pits with sea water to check for leaks prior to future operations
event.summary: On 4 September 2023 at approximately 13:20, approximately 24 barrels of Synthetic Based Mud (SBM) was discharged into the Gulf of Mexico from Alaminos Canyon (AC) 857, H&P 205 Drilling Rig located on the Shell Perdido Spar. The discharge resulted from obstructions in two valves (knife valve and butterfly valve) on the degasser tank discharge line that prevented proper sealing. The knife valve was obstructed by cuttings and the butterfly valve was blocked by a piece of rubber, both preventing full closure despite visual verification by crew members.
recommendations: Crews will fill processing pits with sea water to check for any leaks through containment valves prior to future operations
Install skillet on processing pit master dump valve to prevent unintentional discharge, consistent with active pit master dump valve design

threats.name: Inadequate valve functional testing prior to operation
Visual verification of valve position insufficient due to height and obstruction

consequences.name: Unauthorized discharge of synthetic based mud to Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Fluid Transfer Procedure - Valve Configuration Visual Check
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring visual verification of valve alignment by two personnel prior to fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to verify valve configuration before operations

supporting_text: The H&P Fluid Transfer Procedure states, 'prior to transferring fluids, the valve configuration is to be visually checked by 2 people.'
The H&P Pit-Hand, and the H&P Assistant Driller (AD) both walked down the line up and checked the valve alignments.
The knife valve is also located overhead at a height of about 12 feet, the blade position may have been difficult to verify given the height

controls.name: Knife Valve - Discharge Line Outlet
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent fluid discharge from degasser tank
controls.lod_basis: Primary containment valve on degasser tank discharge line

supporting_text: The knife valve located on the discharge line of the degasser tank, and the butterfly valve used as a master dump valve for the discharge lines connected to the processing tanks were found to have leaked.
The knife valve, according to the H&P drilling crew, was found to have been obstructed by cuttings keeping it from seating and sealing properly.

Both valves had not been functioned and operated immediately prior to the fluid transfer, they were left in the closed position after the last flushing of the processing tanks. The last time the valves were functioned was April 2023.

controls.name: Butterfly Valve - Master Dump Valve
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintended discharge from processing pits
controls.lod_basis: Master dump valve for discharge lines connected to processing tanks

supporting_text: The butterfly valve was observed to have a piece of rubber of an unknown origin preventing full closure.
The butterfly valve was previously locked in the closed position after the processing tanks were cleaned out.
However, the rubber blocking the valve from properly closing allowed the SBM to leak past the butterfly valve to the cuttings shoot and ultimately discharging into the Gulf of Mexico.

controls.name: Valve Locking Mechanism - Butterfly Valve
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent unauthorized valve operation
controls.lod_basis: Lock restricts access to valve operation; only Tool-pusher and Rig Manager have key

supporting_text: The Tool-pusher and the Rig Manager are the only personnel with access to the key and authority to remove the lock.

controls.name: Skillet on Active Pit Master Dump Valve
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unintentional discharge from active pit
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier preventing accidental valve operation

supporting_text: A skillet is installed on the active pit master dump valve to prevent unintentional discharge but, there was no skillet on the processing pit master dump valve (butterfly valve).

controls.name: Pit Level Monitoring and Observation
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify fluid loss from processing pits
controls.lod_basis: Driller monitored pit level sensor and rig manager posted observer to watch for sheens

supporting_text: Approximately 7 minutes after the transfer pump was turned off, the H&P Driller noticed the volume indicator for the degasser tank was decreasing.
The driller monitored the pit level sensor for a minute to see if the volume was just leveling out, but when it kept dropping, he contacted the H&P Rig Manager.

Once the H&P drilling crew identified that the fluid was released into the Gulf of Mexico, the H&P Rig Manager posted an observer to watch for any sheens caused by the SBM discharge. The observer watched for 30 minutes and did not identify any sheens resulting from the discharge.

controls.name: Degasser Tank Design - Slanted Bottom
controls.barrier_role: Engineering design feature to allow solids to settle
controls.lod_basis: Tank geometry intended to separate solids from fluid

supporting_text: The Bottom of the degasser tank is slanted with the outlet valve at the top of the slant, this allows solids to sink to the bottom of the slant. The slant also causes fluid and solids to remain in the bottom of the tank below the outlet valve. The fluid in the bottom of the processing pits made it appear the degasser tank was holding fluid when it was not.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
